Professional Summary

Excellence in Head & Neck Oncologic Surgery

Dr. Mukesh Kumar Sah is a highly trained Head & Neck Oncologic Surgeon with comprehensive expertise in the surgical management of malignancies affecting the oral cavity, thyroid, salivary glands, larynx, and hypopharynx.

Trained at the prestigious Tata Memorial Centre in Mumbai — India's foremost comprehensive cancer center — Dr. Sah brings world-class surgical standards and evidence-based oncologic principles to his patients in Nepal. His experience spans advanced oncologic resections, reconstructive techniques including microvascular surgery, and multidisciplinary tumor board participation.

An accomplished academic surgeon, Dr. Sah is committed to mentoring future surgeons and contributing to the advancement of head & neck oncology through research and national-level academic engagement.

Surgical Specializations

Core Expertise

Click any specialty below to learn more from trusted medical references. Each area represents a core component of Dr. Sah's surgical practice.

🫦
↗
01
Oral Cavity Cancer Surgery

Advanced T3–T4 resections of oral cavity malignancies with attention to oncologic margins, function preservation, and quality of life outcomes.

→ Cancer.org reference
🔬
↗
02
Neck Dissection

Selective, modified radical, and radical neck dissections — tailored to staging and disease extent for optimal oncologic control.

→ ENTnet.org reference
🦋
↗
03
Thyroid Surgery

Complete management of benign and malignant thyroid disease including central compartment lymph node clearance for differentiated cancers.

→ Thyroid.org reference
🧬
↗
04
Salivary Gland Surgery

Superficial and total parotidectomy with meticulous facial nerve identification and preservation for both benign and malignant tumors.

→ Cancer.org reference
🗣️
↗
05
Laryngeal & Hypopharyngeal Surgery

Surgical management with a multidisciplinary approach to preserve voice and swallowing function after oncologic resection.

→ Cancer.org reference
✂️
↗
06
Reconstructive Surgery

Pedicled flap (PMMC, local flaps) and microvascular free-flap techniques to restore form and function after oncologic resection.

→ Microsurgery reference

"Every patient deserves world-class oncologic care, delivered with compassion and precision."
